이사야 23:1.content: The burden of Tyre. Howl, you ships of Tarshish! For it is laid waste, so that there is no house, no entering in. From the land of Kittim it is revealed to them.

이사야 23:2.content: Be still, you inhabitants of the coast, you whom the merchants of Sidon that pass over the sea, have replenished.

이사야 23:3.content: On great waters, the seed of the Shihor, the harvest of the Nile, was her revenue. She was the market of nations.

이사야 23:4.content: Be ashamed, Sidon; for the sea has spoken, the stronghold of the sea, saying, “I have not travailed, nor given birth, neither have I nourished young men, nor brought up virgins.”

이사야 23:5.content: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish at the report of Tyre.

이사야 23:6.content: Pass over to Tarshish! Wail, you inhabitants of the coast!

이사야 23:7.content: Is this your joyous city, whose antiquity is of ancient days, whose feet carried her far away to travel?

이사야 23:8.content: Who has planned this against Tyre, the giver of crowns, whose merchants are princes, whose traders are the honorable of the earth?

이사야 23:9.content: Yahweh of Armies has planned it, to stain the pride of all glory, to bring into contempt all the honorable of the earth.

이사야 23:10.content: Pass through your land like the Nile, daughter of Tarshish. There is no restraint any more.

이사야 23:11.content: He has stretched out his hand over the sea. He has shaken the kingdoms. Yahweh has ordered the destruction of Canaan’s strongholds.

이사야 23:12.content: He said, “You shall rejoice no more, you oppressed virgin daughter of Sidon. Arise, pass over to Kittim. Even there you will have no rest.”

이사야 23:13.content: Behold, the land of the Chaldeans. This people didn’t exist. The Assyrians founded it for those who dwell in the wilderness. They set up their towers. They overthrew its palaces. They made it a ruin.

이사야 23:14.content: Howl, you ships of Tarshish, for your stronghold is laid waste!

이사야 23:15.content: It will come to pass in that day that Tyre will be forgotten seventy years, according to the days of one king. After the end of seventy years it will be to Tyre like in the song of the prostitute.

이사야 23:16.content: Take a harp; go about the city, you prostitute that has been forgotten. Make sweet melody. Sing many songs, that you may be remembered.

이사야 23:17.content: It will happen after the end of seventy years that Yahweh will visit Tyre. She will return to her wages, and will play the prostitute with all the kingdoms of the world on the surface of the earth.

이사야 23:18.content: Her merchandise and her wages will be holiness to Yahweh. It will not be treasured nor laid up; for her merchandise will be for those who dwell before Yahweh, to eat sufficiently, and for durable clothing.
